_meta.description: Bangalore ground-level economic indicators: office real estate, consumer economy proxies
_meta.how_to_update: JLL and CBRE publish quarterly BLR office market reports (usually 3-4 weeks after quarter end). Check jll.co.in and cbre.co.in for 'Bengaluru Office Market'.

commercial_realestate.note: 72% of leases are renewals not new expansions — consolidation signal

corridors.name: Outer Ring Road
corridors.note: Primary IT corridor. Vacancy up from 12% in 2023.

corridors.name: Whitefield
corridors.note: Highest vacancy in major corridor. Several large blocks unoccupied.

corridors.name: Electronic City
corridors.note: Some anchor tenants have downsized floors.

corridors.name: Hebbal / Manyata
corridors.note: Relatively stable. MNC captives anchor this micro-market.

residential_demand.label: Residential demand index
residential_demand.note: Prices still rising on low supply, but transaction volumes softening — affordability squeeze

vehicle_registrations.label: New vehicle registrations (BLR RTO)
vehicle_registrations.note: Mild softening in vehicle registrations — proxy for IT disposable income and job confidence

fb_index.label: F&B revenue index (Koramangala / Indiranagar)
fb_index.note: Weekend footfall remains healthy. Weekday lunch activity slightly down.

upskilling_enrollments.label: Upskilling enrollment spike
upskilling_enrollments.note: Counter-intuitive anxiety signal — spikes indicate workforce fear, not confidence. BLR cohorts specifically up 32% YoY.
